summary: SWE-agent’s tailored interface appears to improve software-engineering performance in a bounded way by structuring search and editing, but the gains remain limited by frequent edit failures and low absolute success on full SWE-bench.

bounded_claims.claim_text: SWE-agent with GPT-4 Turbo achieved a substantially higher resolve rate on SWE-bench than the prior reported non-interactive retrieval-augmented baseline.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence supports the performance comparison, but it does not isolate which specific interface elements caused the improvement, and the absolute solve rate remains low.

bounded_claims.claim_text: On SWE-bench Lite, the LM-tailored agent-computer interface outperformed both a shell-only agent and a retrieval-augmented setup with GPT-4 Turbo.
bounded_claims.bounds: The result supports a bounded interface advantage on this benchmark slice, but it does not fully isolate the causal contribution of each interface feature.
